transcript:
HCP: hi, thanks for coming in, can you tell me what brought you here today
Patient: yeah, so basically i've been feeling really anxious for months now, like my heart is always pounding
HCP: sounds tough, what does that anxiety look like in your daily life
Patient: well i avoid leaving the house, i can't concentrate at work, and i've started drinking more to calm down
HCP: how often do you drink and when does it stop bothering you
Patient: probably 3 or 4 times a week, usually stops after an hour or two but then i'm awake all night
HCP: and that lack of sleep, how does it affect your mood
Patient: i feel irritable, angry at everyone, and i can't seem to focus on reading a book either
HCP: do you feel this is related to the alcohol or just the anxiety
Patient: i'm not sure, maybe it's the drinking making me feel worse about everything
HCP: okay, thank you for being open about that, we can work on both issues together

behaviour.evidence_span: i avoid leaving the house

mood.evidence_span: i feel irritable, angry at everyone

thought.text: poor concentration, racing thoughts implied by anxiety
thought.evidence_span: i can't concentrate at work

cognition.evidence_span: i can't concentrate at work

insight.text: partial insight regarding alcohol and mood connection
insight.evidence_span: maybe it's the drinking making me feel worse about everything

transcript:
HCP: hello, take a seat. thanks for coming in to see me today
Patient: yeah, thanks. been feeling really anxious lately
HCP: can you tell me a bit about that anxiety
Patient: it's just constant worry, like something bad is going to happen
HCP: and has it affected your daily functioning
Patient: yeah, i've been avoiding phone calls and social stuff
HCP: how's your sleep been then
Patient: terrible, i can't switch my brain off, just tossing and turning
HCP: do you feel like you're losing control
Patient: kinda, it's hard to concentrate on work or anything
HCP: and do you have any thoughts of hurting yourself
Patient: no, never, just scared of making mistakes
HCP: good. do you have any idea why this is happening
Patient: i think it's stress, but i don't know how to stop it

transcript:

Patient: hi dr smith, thanks for seeing me

Patient: i can't focus on the screen, just blank out after a few minutes
HCP: and your sleep, how's that been
Patient: terrible, i'm up most of the night worrying about deadlines

Patient: yeah i get really restless when i'm anxious, pacing around the house
HCP: how would you describe your mood right now
Patient: anxious, on edge, like i'm waiting for something bad to happen

transcript:
HCP: hi, i'm dr lee, psych registrar, how are you today
Patient: hi, thanks for seeing me, honestly i'm a bit anxious about this visit
HCP: take your time, what brings you in today
Patient: well i've been having panic attacks at work, heart racing so bad i had to leave
HCP: that sounds really distressing, and is there anything else troubling you
Patient: yeah, i've also lost my job because of it, feel useless now, can't stop shaking
HCP: so you're dealing with the panic attacks and the job loss too, correct
Patient: yes, exactly, i'm scared i'll have another one anytime soon
HCP: and do you have any thoughts of hurting yourself or ending it all
Patient: no no, not really, just want to hide away and stop everything

behaviour.evidence_span: can't stop shaking

mood.evidence_span: honestly i'm a bit anxious about this visit

thought.evidence_span: scared i'll have another one anytime soon

insight.text: good insight into panic attacks and job impact
insight.evidence_span: i've been having panic attacks at work

transcript:
HCP: hi there, thanks for coming in, how are you going today
Patient: yeah, okay i guess, just tired honestly
HCP: i hear you, can you tell me what's been bothering you lately
Patient: well, it's the noise, i can't hear anything properly, sounds like a drill going on in my head
HCP: that sounds really distressing, does it happen all day or just sometimes
Patient: most of the time, i try to ignore it but then i get so anxious about work
HCP: and what about your sleep, are you managing to rest at night
Patient: no, i wake up at 3am and just stare at the ceiling, mind just won't stop
HCP: that sounds exhausting, and how is your mood feeling these days
Patient: pretty flat, i don't feel like crying or laughing, just numb
HCP: i see, and do you feel like you're losing touch with reality or hearing voices
Patient: no, just the noise, and i'm worried i'm going crazy, maybe i should see a specialist

transcript:
HCP: hello, thanks for coming in, i'm dr lee, can i take a seat?
Patient: sure, hi doctor, yeah please go ahead
HCP: thanks, so what brings you in today?
Patient: i just feel really anxious all the time, like my heart is pounding and i can't stop shaking
HCP: that sounds very distressing, when does this usually happen?
Patient: it's been happening for a few months, mostly when i have to go to work or deal with people
HCP: and how has your sleep been during this time?
Patient: terrible, i wake up multiple times and can't get back to sleep, my mind just races
HCP: i notice you're holding your hands tightly, are you feeling jittery or on edge right now?
Patient: yes, i feel so tense, like i'm waiting for something bad to happen
HCP: do you feel like you're losing your grip on things or is it just the anxiety?
Patient: i guess it's mostly the anxiety, i know it's not physical but i can't explain it
HCP: do you think you understand why you're here today?
Patient: yes, i need to get better, i don't want to be like this anymore

transcript:
HCP: hi there, thanks for seeing me today, how are you doing
Patient: okay i guess, just tired all the time really
HCP: and what brings you in today
Patient: yeah just really can't concentrate, like i'm on autopilot
HCP: that's tough, how long has this been going on
Patient: probably about three weeks now, since work got busy
HCP: and does it affect your sleep or appetite
Patient: yeah sleep is all messed up, appetite's gone too
HCP: do you feel sad or anxious about it
Patient: no not really sad, just kinda numb you know
HCP: sounds like you're struggling to cope with daily tasks
Patient: yeah i can't do my job properly, feel like quitting soon
HCP: have you thought about hurting yourself at all
Patient: no never, just want to give up on life basically
HCP: good to know, do you have any family support right now
Patient: yeah partner's there but they're stressed too
HCP: and do you know what might be causing this
Patient: i guess just too much stress, but don't know how to fix it
HCP: we can work on that, how's your memory been
Patient: okay i think, just forget things easily sometimes
HCP: and is your speech any different
Patient: nah normal, just quiet maybe
HCP: do you make eye contact comfortably
Patient: yeah mostly, though sometimes look down
HCP: any changes in your movement or energy levels
Patient: yeah moving slow, like everything takes forever
HCP: and how would you describe your mood today
Patient: flat, just blank inside most of the time
HCP: and your emotional expression seems consistent with that
Patient: yeah not really crying or laughing much lately
HCP: do you understand why we're talking about this
Patient: yeah i know i need help, but scared it won't work
HCP: we'll see, do you have any other concerns
Patient: no just this fatigue and brain fog thing

transcript:

transcript:
HCP: hi there, thanks for making it in, i'm dr lee
Patient: yeah hi, thanks for seeing me, sorry i'm a bit nervous
HCP: that's completely normal, we're here to help you sort things out
Patient: right, yeah, i just feel really scattered lately, can't focus on anything
HCP: tell me more about that feeling of being scattered, what does it look like
Patient: well, i keep forgetting to turn the lights off, and i miss conversations in the shop
HCP: sounds like your attention is drifting a lot, and how has your energy been
Patient: terrible, i used to walk the dog but now i just sit on the couch all day
HCP: so that's a change in your activity levels, and are you feeling down too
Patient: yeah, i feel really heavy and empty, like nothing matters anymore
HCP: do you ever have thoughts of hurting yourself because of that
Patient: not really, just tired and sad, i guess i just want to sleep forever
HCP: i notice you're answering a bit quietly today, is your speech feeling slow
Patient: yeah, i feel like i'm moving in slow motion, everything takes so long
HCP: do you have any idea why this is happening to you right now
Patient: i think i'm just depressed again, but i don't know if pills will work for me

transcript:
HCP: hi, welcome, please have a seat, i'm dr aris, psych registrar
Patient: thanks, yeah, just sit down anywhere
HCP: okay, so what brings you here today
Patient: well, it's mostly the panic attacks, i feel like my heart is going to explode
HCP: that sounds very frightening, how often do these happen
Patient: like three or four times a week, usually when i'm at work
HCP: and what happens during the attack
Patient: i start sweating, shaking, and i feel like i'm going to die
HCP: and you mentioned anxiety before, how has that been
Patient: it's been constant, i can't stop worrying about things going wrong
HCP: right, and regarding your daily functioning, how is your concentration
Patient: it's terrible, i can't focus on reading or watching tv
HCP: i notice you're moving quite fast, is that how you feel
Patient: yeah, i can't sit still, i have to keep pacing around
HCP: do you think these symptoms are related to work stress
Patient: i know it's work, i just don't see a point in keeping up
HCP: that's an important insight, how do you see your illness now
Patient: i think it's a chemical imbalance, but i'm scared of the meds

transcript:

Patient: hey, honestly pretty rough, feeling absolutely drained
HCP: sounds tough, what's been going on lately
Patient: just can't seem to get out of bed, and the noise in my head is getting louder
HCP: yeah, sounds like anxiety is really kicking in, what does that noise feel like
Patient: like a million bees buzzing, makes it hard to focus on anything
HCP: and how has your sleep been during all this
Patient: terrible, waking up at 4am and just staring at the ceiling
HCP: that must make mornings really hard, do you feel guilty about that
Patient: yeah, i know i should be doing more but i just feel worthless
HCP: i hear you, it sounds like low mood is sitting heavy on you too
Patient: exactly, everything feels grey and hopeless lately

Patient: maybe some meds to quiet the buzzing and boost my energy
HCP: good idea, let's talk to the team about that

Patient: well i'm avoiding going to the shops, i don't leave the house much
HCP: do you feel guilty about avoiding things
Patient: no not really, i just want to be safe, i know it's silly but i can't help it
HCP: that's understandable, have you noticed any other changes in your sleep or appetite
Patient: yeah i'm waking up early, feeling tired all the time, and i've lost my appetite
HCP: do you have any history of similar issues before
Patient: not really, this is the first time i'm feeling this bad
HCP: good to know, any family history of mental health conditions
Patient: i don't think so, my parents are fine
HCP: thanks for coming in, let's talk about treatment options
Patient: i'd like to try therapy, maybe medication too if needed
